sections.label: Integrations
sections.title: Integrations
paragraphs: ICOS integrations define how the product connects trusted inputs, identity sources, governed services, model routing, and future external systems without weakening the core architecture.
The integration layer extends capability while preserving continuity, permissions, model identity, privacy, and user authority.
Each integration must strengthen the product system rather than fragment ICOS into disconnected tools, marketplaces, or isolated assistant features.

sections.label: Vault Integration
sections.title: Vault Integration
paragraphs: Vault integration connects ICOS with durable knowledge structures, preserved reflections, source records, and governed continuity material.
This allows profile memory, user-authored material, and future model training sources to remain organized instead of scattered across disconnected surfaces.
The vault layer supports long-term review, provenance, and canonical personal model formation without reducing the product to simple storage.

sections.label: Model Integration
sections.title: Model Integration
paragraphs: Model integration allows ICOS to route interaction through suitable intelligence layers while preserving product-level governance, continuity, and user authority.
The system can evolve across model providers, local models, the canonical personal model, and future dedicated infrastructure without changing the user-facing continuity logic.
This keeps ICOS flexible at the intelligence layer while maintaining a stable product identity and a governed path toward model economy readiness.

sections.label: Identity Integration
sections.title: Identity Integration
paragraphs: Identity integration governs how profiles, model identities, access states, visibility settings, and verification signals connect across the product.
It protects the boundary between user-owned continuity, private model identity, public discovery surfaces, and future marketplace readiness.
This makes identity a governed product system rather than a passive account layer or a decorative profile label.

sections.label: Publishing Integration
sections.title: Publishing Integration
paragraphs: Publishing integration connects selected profile, knowledge, or model-facing material to public surfaces only where permissions allow it.
The product can support discovery, presentation, and controlled exposure without making private continuity, private model identity, provider routing, or source evidence public by default.
This creates a clean bridge between internal reflection, canonical personal model formation, and external publication under owner authority.

sections.label: Model Economy Integration
sections.title: Model Economy Integration
paragraphs: Model Economy integration connects profile, canonical personal model formation, model registry, entitlement, permissions, dashboard, provider routing, capability readiness, anti-abuse readiness, and blocked-until-review marketplace readiness into one governed system.
This prevents the economy layer from becoming a separate marketplace product detached from ICOS continuity and user authority.

The integration makes the model economy a future governed extension of canonical personal models: a blocked-until-review pathway for approved models to become discoverable, hireable, monetizable, or economically coordinated only after strict legal, governance, privacy, security, finance, product, impersonation, and anti-abuse review.

sections.label: Provider & API Integration
sections.title: Provider & API Integration
paragraphs: Provider and API integration allows models to operate through controlled runtime routes while preserving user trust, model identity, cost boundaries, and private infrastructure controls.
The product may communicate capability, but it must not expose provider routes, token authority, private serials, source authorization evidence, or operational routing details on public surfaces.
This keeps ICOS adaptable across providers while protecting model identity, security, and future economic legitimacy.

sections.label: Ownership & Permission Integration
sections.title: Ownership & Permission Integration
paragraphs: Ownership and permission integration ensures that every profile, canonical personal model, public surface, and future economy state remains tied to the user’s authority.

ICOS must distinguish private, public where approved, searchable, interactable, premium-depth enabled, anti-abuse review blocked, impersonation review blocked, restricted, appeal pending, legacy-enabled, and posthumous-continuity blocked states.
This prevents future discovery, hiring, or monetization from bypassing consent, identity governance, dignity protection, or owner control.

sections.label: Capability Profile Integration
sections.title: Capability Profile Integration
paragraphs: Capability profile integration connects what a model knows, how it was trained, which sources support it, and what it is allowed to do.
A future model economy needs structured capability signals rather than vague claims of intelligence, expertise, or professional replacement.
This keeps model discovery and readiness commercially useful while preserving provenance, reliability, and regulated-domain boundaries.

sections.label: Model Population Awareness Integration
sections.title: Model Population Awareness Integration
paragraphs: Model Population Awareness integration is the future platform-awareness layer for canonical model records inside ICOS.
It should derive model counts and states from canonical records, not from public page projections or decorative analytics.

This allows ICOS to understand its model-governance population across private, public where approved, verified, premium-depth enabled, device-integrity review blocked, impersonation review blocked, restricted, appeal pending, suspended, archived, and posthumous-continuity blocked states.

sections.label: Model Economy Release Boundary
sections.title: Model Economy Release Boundary
paragraphs: Model Economy integration remains blocked until review for any public marketplace, payouts, public ranking, autonomous inter-model hiring, posthumous economy, raw physical device serial-number collection claims, device-based public reputation scoring, guaranteed-income language, model consciousness claims, or regulated-domain claims.

The integration layer may prepare registry, entitlement, permission, dashboard, provider, capability profile, and model population awareness connections, but it must not imply active commercial marketplace availability.
This keeps the Products page commercially clear, production-ready, and defensible while preserving legal, governance, privacy, security, finance, product, anti-abuse, and implementation discipline.
